articles.title: Horse Racing wants to steer clear of prediction market sites, but is that the wisest strategy?
articles.summary: Horse racing officials are pushing back against prediction market platforms that want to offer betting on race outcomes, worried about regulatory overlap and protecting their existing wagering monopolies. The resistance shows how traditional gambling industries see prediction markets as direct competitors rather than complementary tools, which could limit where and how bettors access horse racing odds outside conventional tracks.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Backlash
articles.summary: Politicians and regulators are pushing back against the growing influence of prediction markets in politics and policy. This backlash could trigger new restrictions or oversight that fundamentally changes how these markets operate and what bettors can wager on.

articles.title: Tennessee HOPE Scholarship funding threatened by rise of prediction markets
articles.summary: Tennessee's HOPE Scholarship program faces potential funding shortfalls as prediction markets grow in popularity and compete for the same revenue streams that traditionally supported educational initiatives. This signals a broader trend where states may need to choose between protecting existing public programs and embracing the tax revenue opportunities that regulated prediction markets offer.

articles.title: Betting on Everything
articles.summary: The explosion of betting markets now covers everything from celebrity divorces to alien disclosure, turning formerly unbettable events into tradeable assets. This democratization of wagering creates new alpha opportunities for sharp bettors willing to research niche markets that bookmakers haven't properly priced yet.

articles.title: ARK Invest Adopts Kalshi to Better Inform Investors
articles.summary: ARK Invest is now using Kalshi's prediction markets to give their investors better insights into future events and market movements. This marks another step toward mainstream finance embracing prediction markets as legitimate information sources, potentially driving more institutional money and credibility into the space.

articles.title: A Valuation of Polymarket (POLY)
articles.summary: Someone's putting a price tag on Polymarket's native token POLY, attempting to figure out what the prediction platform is actually worth beyond its trading volume hype. The valuation matters because it signals whether prediction markets are building sustainable businesses or just riding the crypto gambling wave.

articles.title: Lawmakers Press to Limit Prediction Bets by Policymakers
articles.summary: Congress is pushing to ban or restrict government officials from betting on political outcomes and policy decisions. The move could legitimize prediction markets by addressing the insider trading concerns that regulators have used to justify heavy restrictions on political betting.

articles.title: We Haven’t Seen the Worst of What Gambling and Prediction Markets Will Do to America
articles.summary: A new piece argues that gambling and prediction markets will cause more social damage than we've witnessed so far. If regulators buy this narrative, expect stricter oversight that could kneecap the prediction market boom before it really gets started.

articles.title: Opinion | Trump is right about polls — and the better bet
articles.summary: Trump's calling out polling accuracy again, and this time someone thinks he's onto something about systematic polling errors or methodology flaws. If polls are consistently missing the mark, smart money will keep flowing toward betting markets as the more reliable gauge of actual electoral sentiment.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Changing the Music Industry
articles.summary: Music labels and streaming platforms are starting to use prediction markets to forecast which songs will hit, which artists will break through, and how album releases will perform. Smart money is realizing that fan sentiment and crowd wisdom often beat A&R executives at spotting the next big thing, creating new opportunities for bettors who understand music trends better than industry insiders.

articles.title: An Evening at Polymarket’s Totally Broken Pop-up Bar
articles.summary: Polymarket hosted a pop-up bar event that apparently went sideways, likely featuring technical glitches, payment issues, or other operational failures that left attendees frustrated. When the world's biggest prediction market can't run a simple bar without breaking, it raises questions about whether they can handle the infrastructure demands of processing billions in election bets.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A trader on Polymarket netted close to $1 million by correctly predicting Iran-related events with unusual precision. This highlights how geopolitical markets can create massive profit opportunities for traders with superior information or analysis, though it also raises questions about whether some bettors have unfair advantages.
